records.ety: [L. vegetatus, p. p. of vegetare to enliven. See Vegetable.]
records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Vegetated (?); p. pr. & vb. n. Vegetating.]

defs: To grow, as plants, by nutriment imbibed by means of roots and leaves; to start into growth; to sprout; to germinate.
quotes: See dying vegetables life sustain, See life dissolving vegetate again.

defs: Fig.: To lead a life too low for an animate creature; to do nothing but eat and grow.
quotes: Persons who . . . would have vegetated stupidly in the places where fortune had fixed them.

defs: To grow exuberantly; to produce fleshy or warty outgrowths; as, a vegetating papule.
